Thanks for that overview, Shubhank. The attendance system sounds practical. Could you tell me more about the machine learning model you used for face recognition? What were some of the challenges you faced in achieving that accuracy, and what was your specific role in overcoming them?

Okay. So, I used SCRFD for face detection and Facenet for face recognition. The specific challenges that we faced were occlusion and not being able to capture everyone's faces. And in order to tackle this problem, we used image super resolution as well as uh taking multiple images to create a combined uh confidence score in order to evaluate the uh in in in order to evaluate the final uh confidence score to uh to uh make the decision whether the student is present or not. So, this is how I overcame the given issues.

Thanks for detailing the models and the challenges you faced. Using SCRFD and Facenet is a solid approach. Could you elaborate a bit more on how you implemented that 'combined confidence score' from multiple images, and what your specific contribution was to designing and implementing that particular solution?

Okay. So, uh the way this works is that uh when we take images from multiple angles from the right corner, the left corner, from the center and from various angles, uh it basically makes sure that all the students are at least covered in one of the images. And if the threshold for confidence uh crosses a particular amount, in our case that was set to be 0.4. Uh then we mark the student as present because uh they have had they have an enough similarity with the embeddings present in our DB. And as per my contribution in this project, I was uh I was the first I was the person who created the database and who created the embeddings from the uh from the facial data of the students available.
